description: The README's 'From source' section presents the make-hooks local pre-commit hook as the way to enforce the 300-line + 100% coverage gates. But a bare core repo with the worktree/merge workflow and CI is an equally valid path to the same guarantee, and which one fits depends on circumstances. The hook gives fast at-commit feedback but is per-clone, per-commit-costly, and only behaviourally discourages bypass. A bare core has no working tree, so the working branch cannot be edited directly at all -- the architecture structurally enforces what the hook only asks politely, with CI as the durable gate. The section is recommendation, not mechanism, so it should present both and state the reasons to pick each rather than implying the hook is mandatory.
title: Document bare core repo as an alternative to the local pre-commit hook
